principle: Show only the choices relevant to the current step and progressively disclose more as user advances — all options at once causes decision paralysis
explanation: X.ux|progressive_disclosure: all_options_at_once -> decision_paralysis + cognitive_overload; technique: reveal_choices_per_step -> user_focuses_on_one_decision_at_a_time; transfer_complexity: physical_to_digital (few_buttons -> structured_menus); NO:showing_all_filters_before_search NO:feature_tour_on_first_launch eg: "search_page_with_20_filter_types_visible" >> "google: search_first -> filters_appear_after_results"

principle: When many options are unavoidable highlight recommended choices with social proof — fewer curated options convert better than exhaustive catalogs
explanation: X.ux|choice_curation: too_many_options -> choice_paralysis -> user_buys_nothing; jam_study: 24_varieties -> 10x_fewer_purchases_than_6; cure: highlight_subset_via_social_proof (trending + popular + recommended); netflix: 18min_average_to_choose -> added_trending_now -> faster_decisions; NO:equal_weight_to_all_options NO:no_default_recommendation eg: "200_movies_flat_grid" >> "trending_row_top + personalized_picks + then_full_catalog"

principle: Break complex tasks into smaller sequential steps — each step with few choices reduces cognitive load and increases completion rate
explanation: X.ux|task_steps: complex_task_on_one_screen -> overwhelm + abandonment; break_into: sequential_steps_3-5_fields_each -> user_sees_progress + manageable_decisions; wizard_pattern: step1_basics -> step2_details -> step3_confirm; checklist_onboarding: notion_style -> learn_by_doing_one_task_at_a_time; NO:10_field_form_on_one_page NO:all_settings_on_one_screen eg: "checkout_15_fields_one_page" >> "checkout_3_steps: shipping -> payment -> confirm"

principle: Always pair icons with text labels especially in navigation — icons alone are ambiguous because the same icon means different things across products
explanation: X.ux|icon_labels: icon_alone == abstract -> user_guesses_meaning; heart_icon: like OR favorite OR bookmark OR rate -> no_standard; same_user_different_apps -> different_icon_meanings -> confusion; fix: icon + text_label -> clarity + discovery + recognition; especially_critical: navigation + primary_actions; NO:icon_only_nav NO:assuming_icons_are_universal eg: "bottom_nav_5_icons_no_labels" >> "bottom_nav_icons + text_labels_like_twitter_x"

principle: Use card sorting to organize navigation by users mental models not internal org structure — have users group and label topics themselves
explanation: X.ux|card_sorting: internal_org_structure != user_mental_model; method: write_topics_on_cards -> users_group_them -> users_name_groups -> debrief_rationale; reveals: how_users_expect_to_find_info NOT how_company_organizes_it; open_sort: users_create_groups; closed_sort: predefined_groups; NO:nav_based_on_org_chart NO:guessing_categories eg: "nav: about_us|products|solutions|resources" >> "nav_labels_from_card_sort_matching_user_goals"
